Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Purported Trust; To Determine Invalidity Of Deeds Transferring Property To Purported Trust; For Damages For Financ
Off Calendar Dec-16-2019 Continued To Feb-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Liza de Vries for Petitioner.
Maria Lawless for Sean Do (Respondent).

Ms. de Vries stated that they are still waiting for discovery, that she will not be able to get a stipulation regarding a protective order for the Adult Protective Services records, that lead Counsel has a medical issue and that she is unable to communicate with him. Ms. de Vries also requested a continuance to January for trial setting and stated that the parties have agreed to go to private mediation. Ms. Lawless stated that opposing Counsel had done nothing regarding discovery until June or July, that she was told Mr. O'Sullivan (lead Counsel) may not be coming back in, raised the issue of delay on behalf of the Petitioner and stated that the Petitioner has no evidence. Ms. Lawless also stated that she wants to go to trial and that this has been a tremendous expense to her Client. The Petition to Determine Invalidity of Purported Trust, etc. is continued to February 10, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that it sees a trial in early spring. The Parties are to file a joint status report addressing status of ADR, discovery, what the claims are, disputes of fact, who the witnesses will be, and time estimates of witness testimony, and a time estimate for the trial. Mr. de Vries stated that she filed the estate petition (303246) to determine the issue of heirship. The hearing date of January 7, 2020 in the estate matter is continued to January 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Purported Trust; To Determine Invalidity Of Deeds Transferring Property To Purported Trust; For Damages For Financ
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Continued To Dec-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Dec-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lizabeth de Vries, Esq. and Kelly Dixon, Esq. appearing for David Zhou.
Maria Lawless, Esq. and Anthony Matricciani, Esq. appearing for and with Sean Do.

The Court addressed the two matters on calendar: the Motion to Expunge Lis Pendens and the Order to Show Cause. The Court addressed the capacity, undue influence, and elder abuse stating that it understood where the suspicion came from. The Court addressed the question of whether the petitioner is an adopted child and addressed the petitioner's expert's focus on the course of events that happened before the formal adoption/without the papers. The Court then addressed the respondent's focus on the documents that speak to Chinese law that parent and child need to live together (the Court noted that the decedent almost immediately left for the United States). The Court addressed further points and stated that it was not inclined to award attorney's fees no matter who wins here. The Court is not compelled to award fees.

Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court took the matters under submission.

The Court stated that the parties should talk to each other about discovery and trial.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR #5229.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Show To Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Dec-03-2019 Denied

Appearances: Liza de Vries for Petitioner.
Maria Lawless for and with Sean Do (Respondent).

Ms. de Vries stated that the matter is not ready to set for trial, that they have an ex parte hearing today and that there is the matter of the adoption that needs to be decided before it goes to trial. Ms. Lawless stated that Ms. de Vries filed an ex parte petition for appointment of a Special Administrator, that she (Ms. Lawless) has lodged the will with the Court showing her Client as the named Administrator, that the matter is ready for trial and that a motion to expunge lis pendens is on calendar for November 20, 2019. The Petition to Determine Invalidity of Purported Trust, etc. is continued to December 3, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a trial setting conference.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Ciaran O'Sullivan, Esq. for David Zhou, opposing party.

Matter called, argument is held.

At the conclusion of argument, Sean Do's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Payment Of Cancellation Fee Compliance With Deposition Subpoenas For Sanctions And For Order Requiring Non Resident Petitioner To File And Undertaking To Secure Costs is taken under submission by the Court. Ms. Lawless submitted a proposed order in open court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: K. Chan. Matter not reported.

Petition To Determine Invalidity Of Purported Trust; To Determine Invalidity Of Deeds Transferring Property To Purported Trust; For Damages For Financ
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Continued To Jun-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan for David Zhou (Petitioner).
Maria Lawless for Sean Do (Respondent).

Ms. Lawless stated that Mr. O'Sullivan e-mailed the amended petition to her yesterday, that they have initiated discovery and that there are a number of depositions that need to be taken. Ms. Lawless also stated that it is her understanding that the Decedent never adopted the Petitioner, that there probably will be a dispositive motion and that she filed her response yesterday morning. The Petition to Determine Invalidity of Purported Trust, etc. is continued to June 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that the parties will move this forward.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
